arguments.task: Open official Attio pages and extract evidence for: product description, API documentation, authentication methods, rate limits, pricing/access tier, and whether an official or existing MCP server exists. Return canonical URLs visited and concise findings with quotes or paraphrases tied to each URL.

raw_result.description: Customizable, data-driven CRM for teams to manage people, companies, deals, and workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Attio documents OAuth 2.0 for multi-workspace applications and API access tokens for single-workspace access, both sent as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header; MCP docs separately note OAuth-based authentication for the hosted MCP server.
raw_result.gating_reason: Attio’s public pricing page lists a Free plan and explicitly includes API and webhook access, App SDK, and MCP server on that self-serve tier.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with documented objects and operations for core CRM data plus webhooks, an App SDK, and a hosted MCP server for AI access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Attio provides an official hosted MCP server documented at docs.attio.com and exposed at mcp.attio.com/mcp.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Attio documents REST API limits of 100 read requests/second and 25 write requests/second; exceeding limits returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. MCP operations also have separate workspace-scoped rate limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are extensive, pricing is public, the free tier includes API access, and Attio operates its own hosted MCP server with documented AI-client integrations.
